proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.1pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Regime stability is 'shifting' on day 1 of Bear — if regime reverts to Choppy quickly, cohort gating will deactivate boost without harm, but watch for whipsaw
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +41.1pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +41.1pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Regime just shifted to Bear (day 1, shifting) so the cohort is now active, but TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental risk and current portfolio Bear exposure is only 2/10 (20%), well below the 80% concentration trigger which is on Choppy. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-14) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Choppy regime (80%) + UP_TRI_SA×Choppy×IT cell (20%) (80.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Choppy regime (80%) + UP_TRI_SA×Choppy×IT cell (20%); recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy and in signal_type=UP_TRI_SA×sector=IT until either Choppy share drops below 0.70 via natural Day 6 exits OR Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes from Candidate to Tier M. Allow new Bear-regime longs only where cohort_health is Tier M+.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would simultaneously invalidate 8/10 positions whose edge sits on an unvalidated Candidate cohort, with no short or hedge book to offset. The UP_TRI_SA×IT cluster amplifies single-sector drawdown if IT leads the rotation down.

evidence: UP_TRI_SA×Choppy×IT cell at 20% share — meets 0.20 cell threshold
Choppy×UP_TRI matched cohort tier=Candidate (n=188) — unvalidated edge
Choppy×BULL_PROXY matched cohort tier=Candidate (n=25) — unvalidated edge
Book is 100% directional long (UP_TRI 6 + UP_TRI_SA 2 + BULL_PROXY 2 = 10/10)
Only Bear-side exposure (UP_TRI×Bear×Other) has Tier M support (n=96) — small offset
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy and in signal_type=UP_TRI_SA×sector=IT until either Choppy share drops below 0.70 via natural Day 6 exits OR Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes from Candidate to Tier M. Allow new Bear-regime longs only where cohort_health is Tier M+.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would simultaneously invalidate 8/10 positions whose edge sits on an unvalidated Candidate cohort, with no short or hedge book to offset. The UP_TRI_SA×IT cluster amplifies single-sector drawdown if IT leads the rotation down.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration triggered at 0.80 (Choppy, at threshold) AND the UP_TRI_SA×Choppy×IT cell hits the 0.20 cell threshold at 20%. The matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=188) — unvalidated edge for the dominant exposure. The book is fully directional long (UP_TRI + UP_TRI_SA + BULL_PROXY = 10/10), so a Choppy→Bear flip would unwind the long-heavy book simultaneously.
